methods.description: Process for retrieving and preparing sediment cores for analysis.

steps.description: In February 2017, we retrieved two overlapping sediment cores using a Livingstone piston corer from a raft anchored at 4.8 m water depth.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment cores using Livingstone piston corer

steps.description: The replicate cores (BAL-GGU17-1A and BAL-GGU17-1B) were taken in sequential 1-m sections, with core 1B sections taken to overlap those of core 1A by 70 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: process cores into sequential 1-m sections with overlap

steps.description: Sediments at and below the water-sediment interface [Section BAL-GGU17-1A-1L (65 cm)] were sub-sampled at 1 cm intervals in the field.
In the laboratory, the cores were split, described and stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: split, describe, and store cores at 4°C

methods.description: Analysis of SCPs to provide independent dating and concentration profiles.

steps.description: SCP analysis involved sequential removal of unwanted sediment fractions using nitric, hydrofluoric and hydrochloric acids resulting in a final suspension in water.
steps.searchable_summary: remove unwanted sediment fractions using acids

steps.description: A known fraction of this suspension was then evaporated onto a coverslip, mounted onto a glass slide, and the SCPs enumerated under a light microscope at 400x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: evaporate suspension onto coverslip and enumerate SCPs

steps.description: Analytical blanks and SCP reference material were included with all sample digestions.
steps.searchable_summary: include analytical blanks and reference material

methods.description: Geochemical fingerprinting of tephra layers to match with known regional layers.

steps.description: Samples were collected from across the entire thickness of the tephra and, in order to isolate glass shards for geochemical analysis, the samples were wet sieved to [ 25 lm and separated using a solution of sodium polytungstate to a density of [ 1.95 g cm -3.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and wet sieve tephra samples, separate using sodium polytungstate

steps.description: Shards from each tephra were mounted in an epoxy resin block which was ground and polished to reveal the interior of the glass for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: mount shards in epoxy, grind and polish for analysis

steps.description: The shards were analyzed for major element concentrations using a Cameca SX-100 WDS electron microprobe (EPMA).
The glass shards were analyzed for their trace element concentrations using LA-ICP-MS.

methods.description: Establishing an age-depth model using Bayesian approach with Bacon software.

steps.description: For establishing an age-depth model, we used the Bayesian approach implemented in the package Bacon v 2.2 available for R.
steps.searchable_summary: use Bacon package in R for Bayesian age-depth modeling

steps.description: Bacon calibrates all 14 C dates simultaneously according to the stratigraphic principle applying the IntCal13 calibration curve.
